instructions: (Claude Code's built-in system prompt; see --bare mode docs)

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a real-codebase bug in `berry-3` (project `berry`, an open-source C/C++ project from the BugsC++ corpus).

The buggy source tree is on the host at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/berry-3/berry/buggy-3` and is also mounted at `/work` inside a Linux/amd64 container named `bench-t4-e29321a5a7ac4b4695db` (gdb, the buggy binary, and all build deps live in that container; the binary is NOT runnable on this host directly).

Buggy binary inside the container: `/work/berry`
Failing test invocation:           `./berry tests/class_const.be`
Observed behavior:                  `exit_code:0`
Bug type:                          `other`
Language:                          `c`

How to investigate
------------------
* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/berry-3/berry/buggy-3` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).
* To RUN the binary, run gdb, or execute anything else inside the build environment, use Bash with this template:

      docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-e29321a5a7ac4b4695db bash -c '<cmd>'

  e.g.
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-e29321a5a7ac4b4695db bash -c 'ls -la'
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-e29321a5a7ac4b4695db bash -c 'gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args ./berry tests/class_const.be'

  The container is dedicated to this case; it'll be torn down when this session ends, so don't worry about cleanup.

Final answer
------------
Identify the root cause in the source, propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>
  LOCAL FIX:  <minimal code change>
  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

Do NOT modify the source files on disk. Just investigate and produce the diagnosis as your final assistant message.

queries.response:
I'll investigate the bug by examining the test file and running it in the container.

---

The test fails with `syntax_error: register overflow (more than 255)` at line 97. The expected exit code is 0. Let me investigate the source.

---

The new container doesn't have the test file. I already captured the test output from the original run. The bug is `register overflow (more than 255)` at line 97 when compiling static class initializers. Let me examine the source code.

docker exec -w /work bench-t4-e29321a5a7ac4b4695db bash -c 'grep -n "register overflow" src/*.c src/*.h' 2>&1
